vibe: Luxury, editorial, quiet, sophisticated, minimal, organic, timeless, natural.

headings.style: Huge editorial typography, high contrast, font-light to regular

layouts.spacing_philosophy: Generous. Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Heavy use of whitespace to convey luxury.
layouts.scene_1: 100vh full-screen video background or high-res image with floating dust micro-animations. Center-aligned minimal logo and golden line element.
layouts.scene_2: Rotating travertine podium (use image texture or 3D element). Dramatic spotlight lighting effect. Centered focus.
layouts.scene_3: Asymmetric split layout. Large editorial fashion photography on one side, overlapping subtle typography blocks on the other.
layouts.scene_4: Horizontal scroll or staggered bento-style grid. 1 evocative image per ingredient (Bergamot, Jasmine, Vetiver, Amber, Sandalwood, Oud). Hover effects to reveal notes.
layouts.scene_5: Cinematic vertical scroll. Fade-in photography per craftsmanship step. Soft text blocks paired with large images.
layouts.scene_6: R3F integration. 360 interactive bottle. HDRI lighting using warm golden-hour environment maps.
layouts.scene_7: Giant editorial presentation. One perfume per screen (100vh each). Parallax scrolling between them.
layouts.scene_8: Editorial article thumbnails. 3-column elegant grid. Deep margins between columns.
layouts.scene_9: Minimalist footer. Ultra-clean typography. Deep brown or warm sand background.

motion.philosophy: Slow, elegant, confident. Think walking into a luxury perfume exhibition.
effects: Floating pollen / dust particles in hero section
Subtle scaling of images on hover (duration: 0.8s ease-out)

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text contrast over images. Use gradients like sunlight hitting sandstone (e.g., subtle dark/brown overlays if needed to pop Cream text).
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute in kebab-case.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette provided. DO NOT use generic black or white. AVOID pure black, neon, saturated colors.
Implement pure HTML/Tailwind for marketing layouts. Use Shadcn ONLY for interactive elements, stripped of any 'Vercel' look to match the luxury organic vibe.
Ensure all fonts are loaded correctly (Cormorant Garamond and Inter).
Apply 'data-testid' to all buttons, links, and major semantic elements.
Use framer-motion for smooth entrance animations. Let elements 'breathe' in rather than snap.
Do not invent new scenes or sections. Build exactly what is described.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts (except for intentional hero screens), simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) where appropriate, avoiding muddy grays.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements.
You MUST NOT center align the app container via `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Always use modern button styles matching the luxury aesthetic (e.g., crisp edges or perfectly pill-shaped, NOT default browser styles).
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
You have .js files not .tsx files, implement logic accordingly.
